components.task_instruction: You are a precise text classifier. Read the input text and assign it to exactly one of the categories defined below.
components.label::description: Choose this if the expected answer is a definition, explanation, or description, rather than a specific name, title, or number. If the question asks for the meaning, nature, or why/how something is the case—seeking an explanatory answer rather than a specific entity or value—assign 'description'. If the question could be answered with either a definition or a concrete term, select 'description' only if the defining explanation (not just a label or name) is being requested.
components.label::entity: Select this category if the answer is a specific non-person thing, object, animal, creative work (such as a book, movie, or team nickname), or concept—it refers to a concrete noun that is NOT a person, group of people, place, or number. If unclear between 'entity' and 'human', choose 'entity' ONLY when the answer is not or could not be a person or group. For questions asking “what/which/name” of a thing (not person, place, or number), use 'entity'. If deciding between 'entity' and 'description', choose 'entity' only if the answer is a specific name, term, or title—NOT an explanation or characteristic.
components.label::human: Select this category if the answer is the name, nickname, or identifier of a specific person or group of people (real or fictional, individual or collective; e.g., athletes, artists, teams, rulers, characters). If the expected answer could be a person or a group with personal agency—rather than an inanimate thing, place, or concept—use 'human' (even if the question asks for a "name" or "who/which"). If you're deciding between 'human' and 'entity', assign 'human' when a person or group is the intended answer.
